text: I start with division of inferior pulmonary ligament after retracting the lung anteriorly.
Next, I dissect the posterior hilum to expose the inferior pulmonary vein, bronchus, and pulmonary artery.
I then concentrate on dissection of pulmonary artery.
Ideally, I want to be able to mobilize the takeoff of the superior segmental branch of pulmonary artery so I can create a tunnel under the posterior fissure.
I retract the lung posteriorly to expose the anterior hilum.
I expose the anterior portion of the inferior pulmonary vein and there's a calcified lymph node here that we worked around.
The patient has an incomplete fissure.
I dissect the tissue between the fissure to get to the pulmonary artery at the level of superior segmental artery takeoff.

The fatty tissue at this location helps identify the pulmonary artery.
Once tunnel has been created, I place an endo leader to help guide the stapler.
The posterior fissure is divided using robot blue load stapler.
I then retract the lung superiorly to expose the inferior pulmonary vein.
I use the robot wide-load stapler to divide the inferior pulmonary vein.
Next, I mobilize and divide the superior segmental branch of the pulmonary artery using the robot wide-load stapler.
I then divide the part of interior fissure with robot blue load stapler.
Next, I dissect around the left lower lobe bronchus and place an endo leader around it.
I use the endo leader to guide the robot green load stapler around it.
I clamp the lower lobe.
and I ensure that the ET tube is mobile and that the left upper lobe can easily inflate.
I then divide the left lower lobe bronchus.
